highlights.title: Show temp target on live activity lock screen
changes: A badge showing an active temp target name now appears on the live activity lock screen widget.
The temp target badge stacks with the existing override badge when both are active.
Badges are moved to the left side of the chart overlay instead of the right.
The chart no longer extends into the future when an override or temp target is active.
The active temp target period is drawn on the chart as a colored band.

sources.title: nightscout/Trio#1180: Add temp target display to live activity lock screen widget
Add temp target display to live activity lock screen widget

highlights.title: Fix watch app back-sync after long staleness
changes: The watch ignores incoming state messages older than 15 minutes to avoid replaying a backlog.
Duplicate or older watch-state updates are skipped so the watch avoids repeated UI updates.
Phone sends now stamp state with the current time so watch-requested re-pushes are not silenced.
Acknowledgment and recommended-bolus messages are still processed and do not show the syncing animation.

sources.title: nightscout/Trio#1190: Fix watch app back-sync after long staleness

Fix watch app back-sync after long staleness * Unify didReceiveMessage + didReceiveUserInfo through one handleIncomingWatchStatePayload * Add guard on wall-clock staleness, then perform monotonicity dedupe * Add state.date with Date() at send time, not build time * Drop lastSent < state.date guard * Watch-requested re-pushes no longer get silenced when no CGM tick has bumped the build-time date Potentially addresses #1162

includedPullRequests.title: Add temp target display to live activity lock screen widget
includedPullRequests.body: When a temp target is active, an orange badge showing the temp target name appears in the top right corner of the widget. Both badges stack vertically if an override and a temp target are active at the same time. I have tried to mirror the existing Override code pattern. PS: screen shots contain an issue that has been fixed already, where the TT badge does not make the graph shift to the left. | Screenshots | Screenshots | |--------|--------| | | | | | |

includedPullRequests.title: Fix watch app back-sync after long staleness
includedPullRequests.body:
* Unify didReceiveMessage + didReceiveUserInfo through one handleIncomingWatchStatePayload
* Add guard on wall-clock staleness, then perform monotonicity dedupe
* Add state.date with Date() at send time, not build time
* Drop lastSent < state.date guard
* Watch-requested re-pushes no longer get silenced when no CGM tick has bumped the build-time date Potentially addresses #1162
